packages.name: flash
packages.synopsis: Package flash is the official Go SDK for the Flash sandbox engine — a self-hosted sandbox platform: claim an isolated sandbox from a warm pool in under 2 seconds, run commands in it, read and write its files, open its live preview, and destroy it.
packages.doc:
Package flash is the official Go SDK for the Flash sandbox engine — a
self-hosted sandbox platform: claim an isolated sandbox from a warm pool in
under 2 seconds, run commands in it, read and write its files, open its
live preview, and destroy it.

Quick start:

	client, err := flash.New() // FLASH_API_KEY + FLASH_BASE_URL from env
	sbx, err := client.Sandboxes.Create(ctx, flash.CreateSandboxOpts{Template: "q1"})
	defer sbx.Kill(ctx)
	res, err := sbx.Run(ctx, "node -e 'console.log(40+2)'")
	fmt.Print(res.Stdout) // "42\n"

The SDK is dependency-free (stdlib only) and every call is context-aware.

funcs.name: IsNotFound
funcs.doc: IsNotFound reports whether err is a 404 APIError.

funcs.name: New
funcs.doc:
New builds a Client. The API key and base URL default from FLASH_API_KEY /
FLASH_BASE_URL; an explicit Option wins. An empty API key is allowed only
for orchestrators running with AUTH_ENABLED=false.

funcs.name: WithAPIKey
funcs.doc: WithAPIKey sets the org API key (`flash_…`). Overrides FLASH_API_KEY.

funcs.name: WithBaseURL
funcs.doc: WithBaseURL points the client at an orchestrator. Overrides FLASH_BASE_URL.

funcs.name: WithHTTPClient
funcs.doc: WithHTTPClient swaps the underlying *http.Client (custom TLS, proxies, tracing).

types.name: APIError
types.doc: APIError is a non-2xx response from the orchestrator.

methods.name: Error
methods.signature: func  Error) Error() string

types.name: APIKey
types.doc:
APIKey is one credential's metadata. The raw key exists only in
CreatedAPIKey.Key at mint time; thereafter only the display prefix is shown.
types.declaration:
type APIKey struct {
	ID         string     `json:"id"`
	Name       string     `json:"name"`
	Prefix     string     `json:"prefix"`
	CreatedAt  time.Time  `json:"created_at"`
	LastUsedAt *time.Time `json:"last_used_at,omitempty"`
}

types.name: APIKeyService
types.doc: APIKeyService manages the org's API keys.

methods.name: Create
methods.doc: Create mints a new named key. Store the returned Key — it is unrecoverable.
methods.signature: func  Create(ctx context.Context, name string) (*CreatedAPIKey, error)

methods.name: List
methods.doc: List returns the org's live keys.
methods.signature: func  List(ctx context.Context) ([]APIKey, error)

methods.name: Revoke
methods.doc:
Revoke disables a key immediately. Revoking the key this client is using is
refused by the server (409) — mint a replacement first.
methods.signature: func  Revoke(ctx context.Context, id string) error

types.name: AssessmentService
types.doc:
AssessmentService is the assessment layer on top of raw sandboxes:
claim a session for a candidate, hand them the playground/preview, then
submit → snapshot → automated scoring. Everything a raw Sandbox can't do.

methods.name: CreateSession
methods.doc:
CreateSession claims a sandbox for a candidate. Hand Session.Token to the
candidate (it scopes them to this one session); keep your org key private.
methods.signature: func  CreateSession(ctx context.Context, opts CreateSessionOpts) (*Session, error)

methods.name: Destroy
methods.doc: Destroy tears the session down without scoring.
methods.signature: func  Destroy(ctx context.Context, id string) error

methods.name: GetSession
methods.doc: GetSession fetches a session by id (operator view, includes the token).
methods.signature: func  GetSession(ctx context.Context, id string) (*Session, error)

types.name: Client
types.doc: Client is the entry point. Construct with New; zero value is not usable.

fields.name: Sandboxes
fields.doc: Sandboxes creates and controls sandboxes (the core surface).

fields.name: Templates
fields.doc: Templates lists and registers sandbox templates.

fields.name: APIKeys
fields.doc: APIKeys manages the org's API keys.

fields.name: Assessments
fields.doc:
Assessments is the assessment layer on top of sandboxes:
candidate sessions with submit → automated scoring.

types.name: CreateSandboxOpts

fields.name: Template
fields.doc: Template is the template id to boot from (required), e.g. "q1".

fields.name: Timeout
fields.doc:
Timeout is how long the sandbox lives before auto-teardown. Zero means
the template's default. Extend later with SetTimeout.

fields.name: Metadata
fields.doc: Metadata is attached to the sandbox and returned on get/list.

types.name: CreateSessionOpts
types.doc: CreateSessionOpts configures a candidate session claim.

fields.name: CandidateID
fields.doc: required — who is being assessed

fields.name: QuestionID
fields.doc: required — the template/question id

fields.name: TimeLimit
fields.doc: zero = template default

types.name: CreatedAPIKey
types.doc: CreatedAPIKey is the mint response — Key is shown exactly once.

types.name: ExecOpts

fields.name: Cwd
fields.doc: Cwd is relative to the sandbox work dir (default: the work dir itself).

fields.name: Env
fields.doc: Env adds KEY=VALUE pairs for this command only.

fields.name: Timeout
fields.doc: Timeout bounds the command (server default 60s, max 5m).

types.name: ExecResult
types.doc:
ExecResult is one command's outcome. A non-zero ExitCode is a normal result,
not a Go error — transport failures and timeouts are errors.

types.name: FileService
types.doc:
FileService reads and writes files inside one sandbox's work dir. All paths
are relative to the work dir; traversal outside it is rejected server-side.

methods.name: Delete
methods.doc: Delete removes a file or directory.
methods.signature: func  Delete(ctx context.Context, path string) error

methods.name: List
methods.doc: List returns every file under the work dir, as relative paths.
methods.signature: func  List(ctx context.Context) ([]string, error)

methods.name: Read
methods.doc: Read returns a file's contents.
methods.signature: func  Read(ctx context.Context, path string) ([]byte, error)

methods.name: Write
methods.doc: Write creates or replaces a file (parent directories are created).
methods.signature: func  Write(ctx context.Context, path string, content []byte) error

types.name: Sandbox
types.doc:
Sandbox is a live (or past) isolated environment. Obtain one via
Sandboxes.Create, Sandboxes.Connect, or Sandboxes.List; then use its methods
to run commands and manage files. Fields are a snapshot from the last API
call that produced it.

fields.name: State
fields.doc: "running" | "submitted" | "timed_out" | "destroyed"

fields.name: AppURL
fields.doc:
AppURL is the sandbox's dev server (http://host:port), reachable while
running. For frontend templates prefer PreviewURL in a browser.

fields.name: PreviewURL
fields.doc: PreviewURL is the authenticated browser preview (frontend templates only).

fields.name: TerminalURL
fields.doc:
TerminalURL is the raw PTY WebSocket; TerminalPage is an embeddable
xterm.js page served by the orchestrator.

fields.name: Token
fields.doc:
Token is the per-sandbox credential for the candidate plane (terminal,
preview, submit). Keep it server-side; hand it only to the end user of
THIS sandbox.

methods.name: Exec
methods.doc: Exec executes an argv directly (no shell interpretation).
methods.signature: func  Exec(ctx context.Context, argv []string, opts ...ExecOpts) (*ExecResult, error)

methods.name: Files
methods.doc: Files accesses the sandbox filesystem (the writable work dir).
methods.signature: func  Files() *FileService

methods.name: Kill
methods.doc: Kill destroys the sandbox immediately. Idempotent.
methods.signature: func  Kill(ctx context.Context) error

methods.name: Refresh
methods.doc: Refresh re-fetches the sandbox's state from the orchestrator in place.
methods.signature: func  Refresh(ctx context.Context) error

methods.name: Run
methods.doc: Run executes a shell command line (via `sh -c`) in the sandbox.
methods.signature: func  Run(ctx context.Context, command string, opts ...ExecOpts) (*ExecResult, error)

methods.name: SetTimeout
methods.doc: SetTimeout resets the sandbox's lifetime to now+d (keep-alive).
methods.signature: func  SetTimeout(ctx context.Context, d time.Duration) error

types.name: SandboxService
types.doc: SandboxService creates, lists, and connects to sandboxes.

methods.name: Connect
methods.doc: Connect attaches to an existing sandbox by id.
methods.signature: func  Connect(ctx context.Context, id string) (*Sandbox, error)

methods.name: Create
methods.doc: Create claims a sandbox — warm-pool hit in well under 2s, cold boot otherwise.
methods.signature: func  Create(ctx context.Context, opts CreateSandboxOpts) (*Sandbox, error)

methods.name: List
methods.doc: List returns the org's sandboxes, newest first. runningOnly narrows to live ones.
methods.signature: func  List(ctx context.Context, runningOnly bool) ([]*Sandbox, error)

types.name: Session
types.doc: Session is a candidate's assessment sandbox.
types.declaration:
type Session struct {
	ID          string    `json:"session_id"`
	Token       string    `json:"session_token"`
	CandidateID string    `json:"candidate_id"`
	QuestionID  string    `json:"question_id"`
	Status      string    `json:"status"`
	AppURL      string    `json:"app_url"`
	PreviewURL  string    `json:"preview_url"`
	TerminalURL string    `json:"terminal_url"`
	ExpiresAt   time.Time `json:"expires_at"`
	// contains filtered or unexported fields
}

methods.name: Result
methods.doc:
Result returns the latest submission's score. ok=false while nothing has
been submitted yet.
methods.signature: func  Result(ctx context.Context) (*Submission, bool, error)

methods.name: Submit
methods.doc:
Submit snapshots the candidate's work, destroys the sandbox, and starts
scoring. Authenticated by the session token (candidate credential).
methods.signature: func  Submit(ctx context.Context) (*Submission, error)

methods.name: WaitForScore
methods.doc: WaitForScore polls Result until scoring completes or ctx expires.
methods.signature: func  WaitForScore(ctx context.Context) (*Submission, error)

types.name: Submission
types.doc: Submission is a scored (or in-flight) submission.

fields.name: Status
fields.doc: "scoring" | "scored" | "error" | …

types.name: Template
types.doc: Template is a sandbox image + its pool/resource config.
types.declaration:
type Template struct {
	ID          string  `json:"id"`
	Slug        string  `json:"slug,omitempty"`
	Title       string  `json:"title,omitempty"`
	Language    string  `json:"language,omitempty"`
	Description string  `json:"description,omitempty"`
	Image       string  `json:"image"`
	MinWarm     int     `json:"min_warm"`
	VCPU        float64 `json:"vcpu,omitempty"`
	MemoryMB    int     `json:"memory_mb,omitempty"`
	PidsLimit   int     `json:"pids_limit,omitempty"`
	// Kind is "api" or "frontend" (frontend templates get a browser preview).
	Kind   string `json:"kind"`
	DevCmd string `json:"dev_cmd,omitempty"`
	// Warm is the live warm-pool depth (list only).
	Warm int `json:"warm,omitempty"`
}

fields.name: Kind
fields.doc: Kind is "api" or "frontend" (frontend templates get a browser preview).

fields.name: Warm
fields.doc: Warm is the live warm-pool depth (list only).

types.name: TemplateService
types.doc: TemplateService lists and registers sandbox templates.

methods.name: Create
methods.doc:
Create registers a new template at runtime; the orchestrator validates the
image exists and starts warming the pool immediately.
methods.signature: func  Create(ctx context.Context, t Template) (*Template, error)

methods.name: List
methods.doc: List returns all registered templates with their live warm depth.
methods.signature: func  List(ctx context.Context) ([]Template, error)

methods.name: Scale
methods.doc: Scale sets a template's warm-pool floor at runtime (the pre-warm knob).
methods.signature: func  Scale(ctx context.Context, id string, minWarm int) error
